acceptance_criteria.text: Constructor uses PHP 8 promotion with three private readonly deps in order via use statements.
proof: use App\Services\OnChainOSPaymentService; use App\Services\KiloClawClientService; use App\Services\TelegramBotRegistrarService;
Constructor: private readonly OnChainOSPaymentService $payment, private readonly KiloClawClientService $kiloclaw, private readonly TelegramBotRegistrarService $telegram

acceptance_criteria.text: Single public method public function deploy(array $request): array.
proof: app/Services/AgentDeployerService.php line 20: 'public function deploy(array $request): array'

acceptance_criteria.text: deploy() returns six canonical keys in every branch.
proof: All 5 return branches (invalid_request, telegram_invalid, payment_failed, install_failed, deployed) include status, stage, agent_name, error, kiloclaw_id, session_id
test_happy_path_deployed_returns_canonical_shape asserts exact six-key shape via assertSame

acceptance_criteria.text: Validation loop across four required keys returns invalid_request/validate branch with canonical-ordered error list, no downstream calls.
proof: Service loops ['agent_name', 'personality', 'telegram_bot_token', 'amount_usd'] in order; non-empty trimmed string check; amount_usd must be positive int
test_missing_required_field_returns_invalid_request asserts error contains 'personality' and all three fake call counts are 0
test_empty_agent_name_returns_invalid_request asserts error contains 'agent_name' and all counts 0
test_zero_amount_returns_invalid_request asserts error contains 'amount_usd' and all counts 0

acceptance_criteria.text: Optional allowlist key accepted but not parsed; missing allowlist is not an error.
proof: Validation loop does not reference 'allowlist'
Happy-path fixture passes 'allowlist' => '' and succeeds

acceptance_criteria.text: Step 1 is Telegram validateToken, before payment and install.
proof: Service body: validation then $this->telegram->validateToken(...) before createCharge/install
test_telegram_token_invalid_short_circuits_at_telegram_validate: getMeCallCount == 1, onchainos and kiloclaw counts == 0

acceptance_criteria.text: validateToken false returns telegram_invalid/telegram_validate branch, skips payment and install.
proof: Branch returns status telegram_invalid, stage telegram_validate, error 'telegram token invalid', kiloclaw_id null, session_id null
test_telegram_token_invalid_short_circuits_at_telegram_validate verifies exact shape and ensures downstream fakes never called

acceptance_criteria.text: Step 2 is payment via createCharge(amount_usd, agent_name) after Telegram succeeds.
proof: Service line: $charge = $this->payment->createCharge($request['amount_usd'], $request['agent_name']);
Call ordering visible via happy-path test call counts all == 1

acceptance_criteria.text: Non-pending charge returns payment_failed/payment branch, install not called.
proof: Service: if (($charge['status'] ?? null) !== 'pending') returns the payment_failed shape with error fallback and propagates session_id
test_payment_not_pending_short_circuits_at_payment: uses OnChainOSException preload so charge returns status=failed; asserts payment_failed and kiloclaw callCount 0

acceptance_criteria.text: Step 3 is KiloClaw install with inline manifest literal.
proof: grep 'spawn.' . strtolower(\$request['agent_name']) matches line 76 exactly once
Manifest array has keys id, name, version '0.1.0', skills []
$install = $this->kiloclaw->install($manifest);

acceptance_criteria.text: Install status not in {ready, booting} returns install_failed/install branch with charge session_id.
proof: Service: !in_array($install['status'] ?? null, ['ready', 'booting'], true) branch returns full six-key install_failed shape with session_id from charge
test_install_not_ready_short_circuits_at_install asserts session_id == 'sess_abc' and error contains 'transport boom'

acceptance_criteria.text: Happy path returns deployed/complete canonical shape.
proof: Service returns ['status' => 'deployed', 'stage' => 'complete', 'agent_name' => $request['agent_name'], 'error' => null, 'kiloclaw_id' => $install['kiloclaw_id'], 'session_id' => $charge['session_id']]
test_happy_path_deployed_returns_canonical_shape asserts exact fixture via assertSame

acceptance_criteria.text: Test file has strict types, correct namespace, extends TestCase.
proof: tests/Unit/Services/AgentDeployerServiceTest.php: declare(strict_types=1); namespace Tests\Unit\Services; use PHPUnit\Framework\TestCase; final class AgentDeployerServiceTest extends TestCase

acceptance_criteria.text: Three in-file fakes implementing the three HTTP client interfaces.
proof: final class FakeAgentDeployerOnChainOSClient implements OnChainOSClient
final class FakeAgentDeployerKiloClawHttpClient implements KiloClawHttpClient
final class FakeAgentDeployerTelegramHttpClient implements TelegramHttpClient

Each fake exposes public nextResponse (or per-method getMeNextResponse/setWebhookNextResponse) that tests mutate. The 'FakeAgentDeployer' prefix is required because the non-prefixed names are already defined in sibling test files within the same Tests\Unit\Services namespace; redeclaring would fatal at load. Spec AC20 explicitly permits 'equivalently named per existing test files'.

acceptance_criteria.text: Each test wires 3 fakes into 3 real services into AgentDeployerService; no PHPUnit mocks.
proof: makeStack() constructs OnChainOSPaymentService, KiloClawClientService, TelegramBotRegistrarService from fakes then new AgentDeployerService($payment, $kiloclaw, $telegram)
grep -c 'createMock|getMockBuilder|MockObject' tests/Unit/Services/AgentDeployerServiceTest.php returns 0
